steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is A, $6x \left(x-12{y}^{2}\right)$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Factor the expression
steps.content: The GCF of $6x^2$ and $72xy^2$ is $6x$. Factor it out:$$6x^2-72xy^2=6x(x-12y^2)$$

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content: The factored form is $6x(x-12y^2)$. A is correct. Distributing $6x$ back through the parentheses checks both original terms: $6x^2$ and $-72xy^2$.

choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — $6x \left(x-12{y}^{2}\right)$: Distributing gives $6x \cdot x - 6x \cdot 12y^2 = 6x^2 - 72xy^2$, which matches exactly. ✓
  • Choice B — $6x \left(x-72{y}^{2}\right)$: Distributing gives $6x^2 - 432xy^2$, not $6x^2 - 72xy^2$. ✗
  • Choice C — $6{x}^{2} \left(12x{y}^{2}\right)$: This is a product, not a difference; it equals $72x^3y^2$, not the original expression. ✗
  • Choice D — $6{x}^{2} \left(72x{y}^{2}\right)$: This equals $432x^3y^2$, not the original expression. ✗
A is correct.
